statement.markdown: Design a binary search function to find the index of a target number in a sorted array of integers. If the target is not found, return -1. The function should handle edge cases such as an empty array or a single-element array.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a sorted array of integers and a target number
statement.outputFormat: The index of the target number if found, -1 otherwise
statement.notes: The function should have a time complexity of O(log n)

examples.explanation: The target number 5 is found at index 4
The target number 6 is not found in the array
The target number 1 is found at index 0
The target number 5.5 is not found in the array

hints.text: Use a binary search algorithm to find the target number
Handle edge cases such as an empty array or a single-element array

editorial.title: Binary Search Function
editorial.intuition: A binary search function should divide the search space in half at each step until the target number is found or the search space becomes empty.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve iterating through the array and checking each element, which would result in a time complexity of O(n)
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a binary search algorithm, which would result in a time complexity of O(log n)
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a binary search algorithm and handling edge cases such as an empty array or a single-element array
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the binary search algorithm will always find the target number if it exists in the array, or return -1 if it does not
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the algorithm would involve dividing the search space in half at each step until the target number is found or the search space becomes empty
commonMistakes: Not using a binary search algorithm

functionSignature.python: def binary_search(self, arr, target: int) -> int:
